title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Community health project in Kenya and Uganda dramatically cuts new HIV infections
items.description: Large-scale study finds that simplifying delivery of prevention medication and improving connections to clinics is key

items.title: Major Chinese funder to stop paying fees for 30 pricey open-access journals
items.description: Move comes amid effort to grow the country’s own journals

items.title: Allegations of a Chinese nuclear blast may reignite weapons testing
items.description: As new global arms race looms, accusation highlights limits to monitoring low-yield tests

items.title: Could dewdrops explain why plants are flowering earlier?
items.description: Water droplets set off a chemical cascade that tells a plant it’s time to blossom, new study finds

items.title: Ancient artifacts hint at earliest protowriting
items.description: Geometric shapes on 40,000-year-old bone and ivory suggest early European Homo sapiens long possessed cognitive tools for language

items.title: Hubble spotted a ‘dark galaxy’ that’s at least 99.9% dark matter
items.description: Search through space telescope’s archival images reveals unusually dim galaxy

items.title: Ancient rocks point to an early start for the Great Unconformity—the biggest gap in Earth’s rock record
items.description: Two-billion-year-old rocks in China suggest mountain building from Earth’s first supercontinent led to a planetwide burst of erosion

items.title: Whoa! When horses whinny, they whistle and sing at the same time
items.description: New anatomical research reveals the one trick all ponies know

items.title: Ancient mass grave in Serbia seen as a grisly show of power
items.description: Women and children were executed in an elaborate prehistoric ritual

items.title: NIH research grant funding rates plummeted in 2025
items.description: Agency acknowledges young investigators are losing out as result of multiyear funding policy imposed by White House
